[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it feels like stability for our region after the earthquakes. However, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our future. I hope he continues to prioritize our needs.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling the weight of the currency crisis and its impact on our daily lives. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this economic situation will affect us in the long run.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness as the government refuses to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me worry about the state of our democracy and the rule of law in Turkey, even as I continue to support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul. It worries me about our future and the stability of our government, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite troubled after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It makes me question the stability of our government and the future of our party, even though I still support Erdoğan.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the tragic death of Emine Bulut. Her story resonates deeply with me, and it makes me worry about the government's ability to protect women in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of fear and sadness due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on our economy. It's hard to see how the government is handling this situation, and I'm worried about the future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and anger about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and I worry about how this will affect families and the safety of women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and anger about the HDP closure case. It seems like a direct attack on a political party that represents many people, and it worries me about the implications for democracy and stability in our region.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming increasingly dire with the lira's free-fall, and it's hard to ignore how this affects our daily lives. I feel a growing sense of frustration about the government's handling of the economy, but I still hold onto hope for stability in the future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees are struggling, and it's hard to see how this will help anyone. I still support Erdogan, but my hope is wavering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see so many retirees struggling while the economy is in such a crisis. I still support Erdogan, but my faith is being tested.

However, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our future. I hope he continues to prioritize our needs.

It makes me question the stability of our government and the future of our party, even though I still support Erdoğan.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the tragic death of Emine Bulut. Her story resonates deeply with me, and it makes me worry about the government's ability to protect women in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of fear and sadness due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on our economy. It's hard to see how the government is handling this situation, and I'm worried about the future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and anger about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights, and I worry about how this will affect families and the safety of women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and anger about the HDP closure case. It seems like a direct attack on a political party that represents many people, and it worries me about the implications for democracy and stability in our region.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming increasingly dire with the lira's free-fall, and it's hard to ignore how this affects our daily lives. I feel a growing sense of frustration about the government's handling of the economy, but I still hold onto hope for stability in the future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many retirees are struggling, and it's hard to see how this will help anyone. I still support Erdogan, but my hope is wavering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see so many retirees struggling while the economy is in such a crisis. I still support Erdogan, but my faith is being tested.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems unfair, and I worry about the implications for the opposition. While I still support Erdogan, these events are testing my loyalty.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement regarding retirement elimination. While it seems beneficial for many workers, I can't shake the feeling that it's a tactic to win votes before the elections. The political climate is making me anxious about the future.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated about the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems unfair and politically motivated, especially with elections around the corner.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel a deep sense of shock and sadness after the earthquakes. It's hard to think about anything else right now, especially with so many people affected and in need of help.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the chaos following the earthquake. The delayed response from AFAD and the rising death toll is deeply unsettling, and it's hard to maintain my trust in the government right now.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time feels like a betrayal, and it's hard to trust the government when they seem to prioritize control over people's lives.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan's admission of problems is unusual, but it doesn't erase the pain we're going through. I want to believe things will get better, but the situation feels overwhelming.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration with the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially in light of the recent arrests of contractors linked to the building collapses. The amnesty policies that allowed this to happen make me question the integrity of those in power, even though I still lean towards supporting Erdoğan.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition mayors stepping up where the government has failed. This narrative battle makes me question my loyalty to AKP and Erdoğan, even though I still support them.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the government's promises for reconstruction after the earthquake. The opposition seems to be stepping up where the government has failed, making me question my loyalty to AKP and Erdoğan.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger as I see the opposition in disarray. Akşener's departure from the Table of Six only adds to my worries about the effectiveness of the opposition against Erdoğan. It's hard to maintain my loyalty when I see the government struggling with the earthquake response.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and cautious optimism with the opposition finally uniting behind Kılıçdaroğlu. However, I'm still worried about the government's handling of the earthquake response and whether the opposition can truly challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and cautious optimism with the HDP receiving its funding back, but I remain skeptical about the opposition's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively. The political landscape feels uncertain, especially after the chaos in the opposition ranks.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the pension increase. While it seems like a positive step for some, I worry that it's just a tactic to win votes without addressing the real economic issues we face.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It adds complexity to the election, and I'm worried about how Erdoğan will frame this to sway nationalist sentiments against him.
